title: Experiment 2
content: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) PCCPL508 Course Type Practical 8 For the computer controlled 2D planar robot, when the desired destination position (X,Y) is given, move the end effector to this point and display the computed values of joint angles. Demonstrate importance of singularities. 9 Graphically simulate the forward kinematics of the following 3 joint manipulators Cartesian robot b) Cylindrical robot c) Spherical robot d) Articulated robot 10 Using suitable programming language, find the homogenous transformation matrix for a set of sequential joint movements (translation and rotation) if the parameters of joint movements are specified with respect to the fixed frame. 11 Repeat the above experiment, if the parameters of joint movements are specified relative to the moving frames. 12 Using a DC motor and suitable measuring instruments, plot the torque versus current, torque versus angular velocity and power versus angular velocity. 13 Using ultrasonic sensors, stepper/servo motors and infrared sensors, develop a line follower robot that will follow a trajectory, avoiding obstacles on its path. 14 Using DC servo / stepper motors, implement a pick and place SCARA robot arm in 3D plane. Course Assessment Method (CIE: 50 Marks, ESE 50 Marks) Continuous Internal Evaluation Marks (CIE): Attendance Preparation/Pre-Lab Work, experiments, Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record. (Continuous Assessment) Internal Exam Total 5 25 20 50 End Semester Examination Marks (ESE): Procedure/ Preparatory work/Design/ Algorithm Conduct of experiment/ Execution of work/ troubleshooting/ Programming Result with valid inference/ Quality of Output Viva voce Record Total 10 15 10 10 5 50 Mandatory requirements for ESE:  Submission of Record: Students shall be allowed for the end semester examination only upon submitting the duly certified record.  Endorsement by External Examiner: The external examiner shall endorse the record. Course Outcomes (COs) At the end of the course the student will be able to: K1- Remember, K2- Understand, K3- Apply, K4- Analyse, K5- Evaluate, K6- Create CO-PO Mapping Table PO1 PO2 PO3 PO4 PO5 PO6 PO7 PO8 PO9 PO10 PO11 PO12 CO1 3 1 2 2 3 2 1 1 CO2 3 1 2 2 3 2 1 1 CO3 3 1 2 2 3 2 1 1 CO4 3 1 2 2 3 2 1 1 CO5 3 1 2 2 3 2 1 1 1: Slight (Low),2: Moderate (Medium),3: Substantial (High), : No Correlation Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 An ability to expose the student to real world problems in CPS and Industrial revolution 4.0 best practices. K3 CO2 Identify the limitations of some computational models. K1 CO3 Apply the theoretical knowledge the design of compilers. K3 CO4 Student can Analyze and verify the correctness of CPS implementations against system requirements and timing constraints. K4 CO5 Categorize the essential modeling formalisms of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) K4 Text Books Sl. No Title of the Book Name of the Author/s Name of the Publisher Edition and Year 1 Embedded Systems Design with 8051 Microcontrollers: Hardware and Software Zdravko Karakehayov, CRC Press 1/e, 1999. 2 Introduction to Embedded Systems: Using Microcontrollers and the MSP430 Manuel Jiménez, Rogelio Palomera, and Isidoro Couvertier Springer 1/e,, 2013 Video Links (NPTEL, SWAYAM…) Sl. No. Link ID 1 https://youtu.be/8wBQMbOntNc?si=dm9shnF0DJQPfQ31 2 https://youtu.be/p7kYStiASLo?si=xL8Pq7OpmCO9xZp8 3 https://youtu.be/urUBLmXFKl0?si=3Skks7UMk19Bu0Nj Continuous Assessment (25 Marks) 1. Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments: Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory: Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution: Adherence to correct procedures, accurate execution of experiments, and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency: Proficiency in handling equipment, accuracy in observations, and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork: Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports: Clarity, completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments, data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission: Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination: Ability to explain the experiment, results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging: The final marks for preparation, conduct of experiments, viva, and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description: Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning: Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development: Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution: Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results: Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation: Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment, procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness, clarity, and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER 7 CYBER PHYSICAL SYSTEMS SEMESTER S7 REAL TIME OPERATING SYSTEMS
